title: Ceriscoides curranii
taxonomy: Ceriscoides curranii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Ceriscoides, which is a part of the larger family Rubiaceae that includes over 600 genera of flowering plants. The plant is native to Southeast Asia and is often found in tropical forests and woodlands. Within the family Rubiaceae, Ceriscoides curranii is classified as a species of flowering plant that belongs to the order Gentianales. The classification of Ceriscoides curranii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Ceriscoides curranii, also known as Philippine gamboge, is a plant species native to the Philippines and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties.
